text:
Once, in a sleepy town tucked between forests and lakes, a fox named Felix liked to explore the old oak trees.

One evening, while the stars twinkled fiercely overhead, Felix decided to climb. Higher.Higher still! But then an owl swooped down with sharp ears. The owl hooted gently.

"Don't go climbing those big branches!" the owl said.

Felix froze.

"But you've climbed them all your life!"

The owl swooped down closer.

Just then a branch snapped. Felix slid down.

"Maybe you shouldn just enjoy watching?"

Felix thought about that.

Night fell early that week.

So each night, just owls.

Now, years went by.

Felix liked some owls.

They were silent observers.

Sometimes their little heads turned sideways.

Those were Felix's favorites.

He liked stories.

Stories... owls.

*He would sit there.

A million years. That's all life seemed.*

The owls didn't talk.

Only thought owlish
